name: This Is What 'Computer Dating' Looked Like In The 1960s
description: Business Insider historical piece recounting the mid-1960s origins of computer dating: the Harvard-originated Operation Match (1965) and its New York follow-on Project TACT (Technical Automated Compatibility Testing) by Lewis Altfest and Robert Ross, running on an IBM 1400 Series computer for $5 per client. TACT spread across NYC before becoming the subject of a 1966 Brooklyn DA grand jury over student questionnaires. Cited as the direct ancestor of today's Match.com.
